---
title: "Gene_Set_Enrichment_Analysis_Cells"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# ============================================
# STEP 1 — Read all six TSV files
# (positive and negative for each
# of your 3 databases)
# ============================================

# Set your base path
# UPDATE THIS to your actual GSEA output folders
cells_hallmark_path <- here("data", "gsea", "2026_cells_H", "HTR8_5um_PFBS_Hallmark")
cells_gobp_path     <- here("data", "gsea", "2026_cells_G", "HTR8_5um_PFBS_GOBP")
cells_kegg_path     <-here("data", "gsea", "2026_cells_K", "HTR8_5um_PFBS_KEGG")

# Read Hallmark results
cells_hallmark_pos <- read.delim(
  paste0(cells_hallmark_path,"/",
         "gsea_report_for_na_pos_1777585400424.tsv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% mutate(database = "Hallmark",
              direction = "Upregulated")

cells_hallmark_neg <- read.delim(
  paste0(cells_hallmark_path,"/",
         "gsea_report_for_na_neg_1777585400424.tsv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% mutate(database = "Hallmark",
              direction = "Downregulated")

# Read GOBP results
cells_gobp_pos <- read.delim(
  paste0(cells_gobp_path,"/",
         "gsea_report_for_na_pos_1777590906516.tsv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% mutate(database = "GOBP",
              direction = "Upregulated")

cells_gobp_neg <- read.delim(
  paste0(cells_gobp_path,"/",
         "gsea_report_for_na_neg_1777590906516.tsv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% mutate(database = "GOBP",
              direction = "Downregulated")

# Read KEGG results
cells_kegg_pos <- read.delim(
  paste0(cells_kegg_path,"/",
         "gsea_report_for_na_pos_1777591765582.tsv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% mutate(database = "KEGG",
              direction = "Upregulated")

cells_kegg_neg <- read.delim(
  paste0(cells_kegg_path,"/",
         "gsea_report_for_na_neg_1777591765582.tsv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% mutate(database = "KEGG",
              direction = "Downregulated")
```

```{r}
# ============================================
# STEP 2 — Combine all results
# ============================================

gsea_cells <- bind_rows(
  cells_hallmark_pos, cells_hallmark_neg,
  cells_gobp_pos,     cells_gobp_neg,
  cells_kegg_pos,     cells_kegg_neg
)

# Check column names
colnames(gsea_cells)
```

```{r}
# ============================================
# STEP 3 — Filter significant pathways (sorted by NES)
# ============================================

sig_gsea_cells <- gsea_cells %>%
  filter(FDR.q.val < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(database, desc(NES))

cat("Total significant pathways:", nrow(sig_gsea_cells), "\n")
cat("\nBreakdown by database:\n")
print(table(sig_gsea_cells$database, sig_gsea_cells$direction))
```
# NES Plots Per Collection 
```{r}
# =============================================================================
# GSEA NES Bar Charts — One Per Collection
# =============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# --- Settings ----------------------------------------------------------------
top_n    <- 20
col_up   <- "#2980B9"
col_down <- "#C0392B"

# --- Prepare data ------------------------------------------------------------
gsea_clean_cells <- sig_gsea_cells %>%
  mutate(
    pathway_label = NAME %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
      str_remove("^GOBP ") %>%
      str_remove("^HALLMARK ") %>%
      str_remove("^KEGG ") %>%
      str_to_title() %>%
      str_wrap(width = 45),
    direction = factor(direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated"))
  )

# --- Plot function ------------------------------------------------------------
plot_nes <- function(df, db) {
  
  plot_df <- df %>%
    filter(database == db) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(NES))) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
    mutate(pathway_label = fct_reorder(pathway_label, NES))
  
  x_max <- ceiling(max(abs(plot_df$NES), na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5)
  
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = NES, y = pathway_label, fill = direction)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_col(width = 0.75, alpha = 0.9) +
    geom_text(
      aes(
        label = paste0("FDR=", formatC(FDR.q.val, format = "e", digits = 1)),
        hjust = ifelse(NES > 0, -0.08, 1.08)
      ),
      size = 2.7, color = "grey30"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("Upregulated" = col_up, "Downregulated" = col_down),
      name = "Direction", drop = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      limits = c(-x_max, x_max),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.25))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(db, " — GSEA Normalized Enrichment Scores"),
      x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
      legend.position    = "bottom",
      plot.margin        = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
    )
}

# --- Generate plots ----------------------------------------------------------
# Check your database values first:
unique(sig_gsea_cells$database)

p_gobp_cells     <- plot_nes(gsea_clean_cells, "GOBP")
p_hallmark_cells <- plot_nes(gsea_clean_cells, "Hallmark")
p_kegg_cells     <- plot_nes(gsea_clean_cells, "KEGG")

print(p_gobp_cells)
print(p_hallmark_cells)
print(p_kegg_cells)

# Save GSEA NES bar plots
ggsave(
  filename    = "Figure_cells_GSEA_GOBP_NES.tiff",
  plot        = p_gobp_cells,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 8,
  height      = 7,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)

ggsave(
  filename    = "Figure_cells_GSEA_Hallmark_NES.tiff",
  plot        = p_hallmark_cells,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 8,
  height      = 7,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)

ggsave(
  filename    = "Figure_cells_GSEA_KEGG_NES.tiff",
  plot        = p_kegg_cells,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 8,
  height      = 7,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)
write.csv(
  sig_gsea_cells,
  file = here("output", "cells", "sig_gsea_cells.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
colnames(sig_gsea_cells)
head(sig_gsea_cells, 3)
```
```{r}
# =============================================================================
# GSEA Heatmap — Hallmark (Main Figure)
# =============================================================================

# --- Filter to Hallmark, ≥2 comparisons --------------------------------------
hallmark_counts <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "Hallmark") %>%
  group_by(NAME) %>%
  summarise(n_sig = n_distinct(comparison), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_sig >= 2)

gsea_hallmark <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "Hallmark", NAME %in% hallmark_counts$NAME) %>%
  mutate(
    pathway_label = NAME %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
      str_remove("^HALLMARK ") %>%
      str_to_title() %>%
      str_wrap(width = 35),
    comparison = factor(comparison, levels = comparison_order)
  )

# Complete grid so NA tiles show as grey
gsea_hallmark_complete <- gsea_hallmark %>%
  dplyr::select(NAME, pathway_label, database, comparison, NES, FDR.q.val) %>%
  complete(nesting(NAME, pathway_label, database), comparison)

# Order rows by mean NES
hallmark_order <- gsea_hallmark_complete %>%
  group_by(NAME, pathway_label) %>%
  summarise(mean_NES = mean(NES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mean_NES)

gsea_hallmark_complete$pathway_label <- factor(
  gsea_hallmark_complete$pathway_label,
  levels = unique(hallmark_order$pathway_label)
)

# --- Plot --------------------------------------------------------------------
p_hallmark_heatmap <- ggplot(gsea_hallmark_complete,
                              aes(x = comparison, y = pathway_label, fill = NES)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = gsea_hallmark_complete %>% filter(FDR.q.val < 0.05),
    aes(label = "*"),
    size = 4, color = "black", vjust = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#C0392B",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    name     = "NES",
    na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hallmark Pathway Enrichment Across Comparisons",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    legend.position  = "right",
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_hallmark_heatmap)

# --- Save main figure --------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  "Figure_GSEA_Heatmap_Hallmark.tiff",
  p_hallmark_heatmap,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 9,
  height      = 8,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)

# =============================================================================
# Supplemental Heatmaps — GOBP and KEGG
# =============================================================================

# GOBP — ≥2 comparisons, top 30 by mean absolute NES
gobp_counts <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "GOBP") %>%
  group_by(NAME) %>%
  summarise(n_sig = n_distinct(comparison), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_sig >= 2)

gobp_top30 <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "GOBP", NAME %in% gobp_counts$NAME) %>%
  group_by(NAME) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs_NES = mean(abs(NES), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_abs_NES)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 30)

gsea_gobp <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "GOBP", NAME %in% gobp_top30$NAME) %>%
  mutate(
    pathway_label = NAME %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
      str_remove("^GOBP ") %>%
      str_to_title() %>%
      str_wrap(width = 40),
    comparison = factor(comparison, levels = comparison_order)
  )

gsea_gobp_complete <- gsea_gobp %>%
  dplyr::select(NAME, pathway_label, database, comparison, NES, FDR.q.val) %>%
  complete(nesting(NAME, pathway_label, database), comparison)

gobp_order <- gsea_gobp_complete %>%
  group_by(NAME, pathway_label) %>%
  summarise(mean_NES = mean(NES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mean_NES)

gsea_gobp_complete$pathway_label <- factor(
  gsea_gobp_complete$pathway_label,
  levels = unique(gobp_order$pathway_label)
)

p_gobp_heatmap <- ggplot(gsea_gobp_complete,
                          aes(x = comparison, y = pathway_label, fill = NES)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = gsea_gobp_complete %>% filter(FDR.q.val < 0.05),
    aes(label = "*"),
    size = 4, color = "black", vjust = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#C0392B",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    name     = "NES",
    na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "GOBP Pathway Enrichment Across Comparisons (Supplemental)",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    legend.position  = "right",
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_gobp_heatmap)

ggsave("Supplemental_GSEA_Heatmap_GOBP.tiff", p_gobp_heatmap,
       path        = here("output", "figures"),
       width       = 9,
       height      = 10,
       units       = "in",
       dpi         = 1200,
       device      = "tiff",
       compression = "lzw")
# KEGG — ≥2 comparisons (small collection, won't be too tall)
kegg_counts <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "KEGG") %>%
  group_by(NAME) %>%
  summarise(n_sig = n_distinct(comparison), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_sig >= 2)

gsea_kegg <- gsea_all %>%
  filter(database == "KEGG", NAME %in% kegg_counts$NAME) %>%
  mutate(
    pathway_label = NAME %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
      str_remove("^KEGG ") %>%
      str_to_title() %>%
      str_wrap(width = 40),
    comparison = factor(comparison, levels = comparison_order)
  )

gsea_kegg_complete <- gsea_kegg %>%
  dplyr::select(NAME, pathway_label, database, comparison, NES, FDR.q.val) %>%
  complete(nesting(NAME, pathway_label, database), comparison)

kegg_order <- gsea_kegg_complete %>%
  group_by(NAME, pathway_label) %>%
  summarise(mean_NES = mean(NES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mean_NES)

gsea_kegg_complete$pathway_label <- factor(
  gsea_kegg_complete$pathway_label,
  levels = unique(kegg_order$pathway_label)
)

p_kegg_heatmap <- ggplot(gsea_kegg_complete,
                          aes(x = comparison, y = pathway_label, fill = NES)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = gsea_kegg_complete %>% filter(FDR.q.val < 0.05),
    aes(label = "*"),
    size = 4, color = "black", vjust = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#C0392B", mid = "white", high = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0, name = "NES", na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "KEGG Pathway Enrichment Across Comparisons (Supplemental)",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.margin     = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_kegg_heatmap)

# --- Save supplementals ------------------------------------------------------
ggsave("Supplemental_GSEA_Heatmap_GOBP.tiff", p_gobp_heatmap,
       path = here("output", "figures"),
       width = 9, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 1200,
       device = "tiff", compression = "lzw")

ggsave("Supplemental_GSEA_Heatmap_KEGG.tiff", p_kegg_heatmap,
       path = here("output", "figures"),
       width = 9, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 1200,
       device = "tiff", compression = "lzw")

# --- CSV export --------------------------------------------------------------
write.csv(
  bind_rows(gsea_hallmark_complete, gsea_gobp_complete, gsea_kegg_complete) %>%
    dplyr::select(NAME, database, comparison, NES, FDR.q.val) %>%
    arrange(database, NAME, comparison),
  file      = here("output", "sig_gsea_heatmap_data.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# GSEA Heatmap — Hallmark with Physiological Groups
# =============================================================================

# --- Pathway group lookup table ----------------------------------------------
pathway_groups <- tribble(
  ~NAME,                                      ~group,
  "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE",           "Immune & Inflammation",
  "HALLMARK_TNFA_SIGNALING_VIA_NFKB",         "Immune & Inflammation",
  "HALLMARK_IL6_JAK_STAT3_SIGNALING",         "Immune & Inflammation",
  "HALLMARK_INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE",       "Immune & Inflammation",
  "HALLMARK_INTERFERON_ALPHA_RESPONSE",       "Immune & Inflammation",
  "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1",                  "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V2",                  "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS",                     "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT",                  "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "HALLMARK_KRAS_SIGNALING_UP",               "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "HALLMARK_MTORC1_SIGNALING",                "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "HALLMARK_GLYCOLYSIS",                      "Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION",       "Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_HYPOXIA",                         "Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_CHOLESTEROL_HOMEOSTASIS",         "Lipid Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_BILE_ACID_METABOLISM",            "Lipid Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_PEROXISOME",                      "Lipid Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_XENOBIOTIC_METABOLISM",           "Lipid Metabolism",
  "HALLMARK_ESTROGEN_RESPONSE_EARLY",         "Hormonal & Endocrine",
  "HALLMARK_ESTROGEN_RESPONSE_LATE",          "Hormonal & Endocrine",
  "HALLMARK_MYOGENESIS",                      "Development & Differentiation",
  "HALLMARK_EPITHELIAL_MESENCHYMAL_TRANSITION","Development & Differentiation",
  "HALLMARK_WNT_BETA_CATENIN_SIGNALING",      "Development & Differentiation"
)

# --- Group factor order ------------------------------------------------------
group_order <- c(
  "Immune & Inflammation",
  "Cell Proliferation & Growth",
  "Metabolism",
  "Lipid Metabolism",
  "Hormonal & Endocrine",
  "Development & Differentiation"
)

# --- Join groups to heatmap data ---------------------------------------------
gsea_hallmark_complete <- gsea_hallmark_complete %>%
  dplyr::select(-group) %>%
  left_join(pathway_groups, by = "NAME") %>%
  mutate(group = factor(group, levels = group_order))

# --- Order rows within each group by mean NES --------------------------------
hallmark_order <- gsea_hallmark_complete %>%
  group_by(NAME, pathway_label, group) %>%
  summarise(mean_NES = mean(NES, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(group, mean_NES)

gsea_hallmark_complete$pathway_label <- factor(
  gsea_hallmark_complete$pathway_label,
  levels = unique(hallmark_order$pathway_label)
)

# --- Plot --------------------------------------------------------------------
p_hallmark_heatmap <- ggplot(gsea_hallmark_complete,
                              aes(x = comparison, y = pathway_label, fill = NES)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = gsea_hallmark_complete %>% filter(FDR.q.val < 0.05),
    aes(label = "*"),
    size = 4, color = "black", vjust = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#C0392B",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    name     = "NES",
    na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  facet_grid(group ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
labs(
  title   = "Hallmark Pathway Enrichment Across Comparisons",
  x       = NULL,
  y       = NULL,
  caption = "All displayed pathways: FDR < 0.1. * FDR < 0.05"
) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    strip.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, angle = 0),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", color = NA),
    panel.spacing    = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    legend.position  = "right",
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 10, 10, 10),
    plot.caption     = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey30", hjust = 0, face = "italic")
  )

print(p_hallmark_heatmap)

# --- Save --------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  "Figure_GSEA_Heatmap_Hallmark_Grouped.tiff",
  p_hallmark_heatmap,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 9,
  height      = 9,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# 1. DOT PLOT — NES color + FDR size
# =============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Only plot significant cells (drop NAs)
gsea_dot <- gsea_hallmark_complete %>%
  filter(!is.na(NES)) %>%
  mutate(neg_log10_fdr = -log10(FDR.q.val))

p_dotplot <- ggplot(gsea_dot,
                    aes(x = comparison, y = pathway_label,
                        color = NES, size = neg_log10_fdr)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low      = "#C0392B",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    name     = "NES"
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name   = "-log10(FDR)",
    range  = c(2, 8)
  ) +
  facet_grid(group ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Hallmark Pathway Enrichment Across Comparisons",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    strip.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, angle = 0),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", color = NA),
    panel.spacing    = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    legend.position  = "right",
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_dotplot)

ggsave(
  "Figure_GSEA_DotPlot_Hallmark.tiff",
  p_dotplot,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 9,
  height      = 9,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)


# =============================================================================
# 2. UPSET PLOT — pathway overlap across comparisons
# =============================================================================

library(UpSetR)

# Build binary matrix (pathways x comparisons)
upset_data <- gsea_hallmark_complete %>%
  filter(!is.na(NES)) %>%
  mutate(
    comparison_clean = comparison %>%
      str_replace_all("\n", " ") %>%
      str_replace_all("µ", "u") %>%
      str_replace_all(" ", "_")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(NAME, comparison_clean) %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = comparison_clean,
    values_from = present,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

rownames(upset_data) <- upset_data$NAME
upset_data <- upset_data %>% dplyr::select(-NAME)
```
```{r}
#installed.packages("UpSetR")
library(UpSetR)
# Print to screen first
# Replace both upset() calls with UpSetR::upset()

# Screen version
UpSetR::upset(
  upset_data,
  sets              = colnames(upset_data),
  order.by          = "freq",
  decreasing        = TRUE,
  mb.ratio          = c(0.6, 0.4),
  text.scale        = c(1.3, 1.1, 1, 1, 1.2, 1),
  point.size        = 3,
  line.size         = 1,
  mainbar.y.label   = "Pathway Intersections",
  sets.x.label      = "Pathways Per Comparison",
  matrix.color      = "#2980B9",
  main.bar.color    = "#2C3E50"
)

# Save version
tiff(
  filename    = here("output", "figures", "Figure_GSEA_UpSet_Hallmark.tiff"),
  width       = 10,
  height      = 6,
  units       = "in",
  res         = 1200,
  compression = "lzw"
)
UpSetR::upset(
  upset_data,
  sets              = colnames(upset_data),
  order.by          = "freq",
  decreasing        = TRUE,
  mb.ratio          = c(0.6, 0.4),
  text.scale        = c(1.3, 1.1, 1, 1, 1.2, 1),
  point.size        = 3,
  line.size         = 1,
  mainbar.y.label   = "Pathway Intersections",
  sets.x.label      = "Pathways Per Comparison",
  matrix.color      = "#2980B9",
  main.bar.color    = "#2C3E50"
)
dev.off()


# =============================================================================
# 3. SUMMARY BUBBLE PLOT — group level
# =============================================================================

group_summary <- gsea_hallmark_complete %>%
  filter(!is.na(NES)) %>%
  group_by(group, comparison) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pathways = n(),
    mean_NES   = mean(NES, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    group      = factor(group, levels = rev(group_order)),
    comparison = factor(comparison, levels = comparison_order)
  )

p_bubble <- ggplot(group_summary,
                   aes(x = comparison, y = group,
                       size = n_pathways, color = mean_NES)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low      = "#C0392B",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    name     = "Mean NES"
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name  = "# Significant\nPathways",
    range = c(3, 14)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hallmark Pathway Group Enrichment Across Comparisons",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x    = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y    = element_text(size = 10, color = "black", face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.4),
    plot.margin    = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_bubble)

ggsave(
  "Figure_GSEA_BubblePlot_Groups.tiff",
  p_bubble,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 9,
  height      = 6,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```





















 
```{r}
# ============================================
# View upregulated pathways first
# (only 20 — manageable to look at all)
# ============================================

cat("=== UPREGULATED PATHWAYS (n=20) ===\n")
up_pathways <- sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Upregulated") %>%
  arrange(desc(NES)) %>%
  select(NAME, NES, NOM.p.val,
         FDR.q.val, SIZE, database)

View(up_pathways)

# ============================================
# View top downregulated pathways
# Sort by most negative NES
# ============================================

cat("\n=== TOP 30 DOWNREGULATED PATHWAYS ===\n")
down_pathways <- sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Downregulated") %>%
  arrange(NES) %>%     # most negative first
  select(NAME, NES, NOM.p.val,
         FDR.q.val, SIZE, database) %>%
  head(30)

View(down_pathways)

# ============================================
# View Hallmark specifically
# Most interpretable database
# Good starting point for story
# ============================================

cat("\n=== HALLMARK RESULTS ===\n")
hallmark_sig <- sig_gsea %>%
  filter(database == "Hallmark") %>%
  arrange(NES) %>%
  select(NAME, NES, NOM.p.val,
         FDR.q.val, SIZE, direction)

View(hallmark_sig)

# ============================================
# SAVE ALL TO CSV — easiest to work with
# ============================================

write.csv(
  sig_gsea %>%
    arrange(database,
            direction,
            desc(abs(NES))) %>%
    select(NAME, NES, NOM.p.val,
           FDR.q.val, SIZE,
           LEADING.EDGE,
           database, direction),
  file = here("output", "cells", "GSEA_significant_pathways.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("CSV saved! Open in Excel to explore.\n")

```
# Creating NES Bar Charts- One Per Collection 

```{r}

```

```{r}
# Get complete list of all pathway names to assign biological themes correctly 
# All upregulated pathways
cat("=== ALL UPREGULATED PATHWAYS ===\n")
all_up <- sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Upregulated") %>%
  arrange(desc(NES)) %>%
  select(NAME, NES, FDR.q.val, database)

write.csv(
  all_up,
  here("output", "cells", "cells_all_upregulated_pathways.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# All downregulated pathways
cat("=== ALL DOWNREGULATED PATHWAYS ===\n")
all_down <- sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Downregulated") %>%
  arrange(NES) %>%
  select(NAME, NES, FDR.q.val, database)

write.csv(
  all_down,
  here("output", "cells", "cells_all_downregulated_pathways.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("Both CSVs saved!\n")
cat("Upregulated pathways:", nrow(all_up), "\n")
cat("Downregulated pathways:", nrow(all_down), "\n")

```
```{r}
# ============================================
# EXTRACT KEYWORDS FROM PATHWAY NAMES
# Helps you see what themes naturally
# emerge from YOUR specific data
# ============================================

# Get all pathway names
all_names <- sig_gsea$NAME

# Remove database prefix
# (HALLMARK_, GOBP_, KEGG_)
clean_names <- all_names %>%
  str_remove("^HALLMARK_") %>%
  str_remove("^GOBP_") %>%
  str_remove("^KEGG_")

# Split into individual words
all_words <- clean_names %>%
  str_split("_") %>%
  unlist() %>%
  tolower() %>%
  # Remove very common uninformative words
  .[!. %in% c("of", "to", "in", "the",
               "and", "or", "via", "by",
               "with", "from", "a", "an",
               "process", "regulation",
               "positive", "negative",
               "involved", "response",
               "dependent", "mediated",
               "pathway", "signaling",
               "activity", "based")] %>%
  table() %>%
  sort(decreasing = TRUE)

# Show top 50 most common words
cat("=== TOP 50 KEYWORDS IN YOUR PATHWAYS ===\n")
print(head(all_words, 50))
```
```{r}
# Print all pathway names cleanly
# so we can verify theme assignment

cat("=== UPREGULATED (n=20) ===\n")
sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Upregulated") %>%
  arrange(desc(NES)) %>%
  pull(NAME) %>%
  cat(sep = "\n")

cat("\n\n=== DOWNREGULATED HALLMARK (n=27) ===\n")
sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Downregulated",
         database == "Hallmark") %>%
  arrange(NES) %>%
  pull(NAME) %>%
  cat(sep = "\n")

cat("\n\n=== DOWNREGULATED KEGG (n=36) ===\n")
sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Downregulated",
         database == "KEGG") %>%
  arrange(NES) %>%
  pull(NAME) %>%
  cat(sep = "\n")

cat("\n\n=== DOWNREGULATED GOBP TOP 30 ===\n")
sig_gsea %>%
  filter(direction == "Downregulated",
         database == "GOBP") %>%
  arrange(NES) %>%
  head(30) %>%
  pull(NAME) %>%
  cat(sep = "\n")
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# ASSIGN BIOLOGICAL THEMES
# Based on exact pathway names in your data
# ============================================

sig_gsea_themed <- sig_gsea %>%
  mutate(

    theme = case_when(

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 1 — Mitochondrial & Energy Metabolism
      # Includes: oxidative phosphorylation,
      # glycolysis, TCA cycle, fatty acid oxidation,
      # neurodegenerative disease pathways
      # (Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, Huntington's
      # all driven by mitochondrial dysfunction)
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION|",
               "GLYCOLYSIS|",
               "FATTY_ACID_METABOL|",
               "ADIPOGENESIS|",
               "CHOLESTEROL_HOMEOSTASIS|",
               "BILE_ACID|",
               "XENOBIOTIC_METABOL|",
               "ATP_SYNTHESIS|",
               "ATP_BIOSYNTHETIC|",
               "ELECTRON_TRANSPORT|",
               "MITOCHONDRIAL_ELECTRON|",
               "MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSLATION|",
               "TRICARBOXYLIC_ACID|",
               "CITRATE_CYCLE|",
               "PYRUVATE_METABOL|",
               "BUTANOATE|",
               "PROPANOATE|",
               "PENTOSE_PHOSPHATE|",
               "FRUCTOSE_AND_MANNOSE|",
               "GALACTOSE_METABOL|",
               "PPAR_SIGNALING|",
               "PROTON_MOTIVE_FORCE|",
               "PROTON_TRANSMEMBRANE|",
               "PARKINSONS|",
               "ALZHEIMERS|",
               "HUNTINGTONS")) ~
        "Mitochondrial &\nEnergy Metabolism",

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 2 — Lipid & Sterol Biosynthesis
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("STEROL_BIOSYNTHETIC|",
               "CHOLESTEROL_BIOSYNTHETIC|",
               "STEROID_BIOSYNTHETIC|",
               "GLYCOSPHINGOLIPID|",
               "GLYCOLIPID_CATABOLIC|",
               "ALCOHOL_BIOSYNTHETIC|",
               "GLYCAN_BIOSYNTHESIS|",
               "PORPHYRIN|",
               "AMINO_SUGAR|",
               "PEROXISOME|",
               "POLYSACCHARIDE_BIOSYNTHETIC|",
               "BIOSYNTHESIS_OF_UNSATURATED_FATTY|",
               "LIPID|",
               "STEROL")) ~
        "Lipid & Sterol\nBiosynthesis",

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 3 — Amino Acid & Xenobiotic Metabolism
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("TRYPTOPHAN|",
               "VALINE_LEUCINE|",
               "GLUTATHIONE|",
               "ARGININE_AND_PROLINE|",
               "METABOLISM_OF_XENOBIOTICS|",
               "AMINOACYL_TRNA|",
               "DOPAMINE_METABOLIC|",
               "CELLULAR_RESPONSE_TO_TOXIC|",
               "CELLULAR_OXIDANT_DETOX")) ~
        "Amino Acid &\nXenobiotic Metabolism",

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 4 — Signaling & Cell Growth
      # Includes mitotic and meiotic pathways
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("MTORC1|",
               "PI3K_AKT|",
               "MYC_TARGETS|",
               "P53|",
               "APOPTOSIS|",
               "ANGIOGEN|",
               "EPITHELIAL_MESENCHYMAL|",
               "INSULIN_SIGNALING|",
               "CELL_CYCLE|",
               "FERROPTOSIS|",
               "APOPTOTIC|",
               "MITOTIC_RECOMBINATION|",
               "SPERMATID_DIFFERENTIATION|",
               "CHROMOSOME_ORGANIZATION_INVOLVED|",
               "PRE_MIRNA|",
               "CELL_PROLIFERATION")) ~
        "Signaling &\nCell Growth",

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 5 — Immune & Inflammatory Response
      # Includes hormone response and
      # sensory/chemical stimulus pathways
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("REACTIVE_OXYGEN|",
               "COMPLEMENT|",
               "COAGULATION|",
               "INTERFERON|",
               "IL6_JAK|",
               "TNFA_SIGNALING|",
               "HYPOXIA|",
               "UV_RESPONSE|",
               "ESTROGEN_RESPONSE|",
               "ANDROGEN_RESPONSE|",
               "SYSTEMIC_LUPUS|",
               "PATHOGENIC_ESCHERICHIA|",
               "RESPONSE_TO_FUNGUS|",
               "ZYMOGEN|",
               "T_CELL_PROLIFERATION|",
               "INTRACELLULAR_IRON|",
               "SENSORY_PERCEPTION_OF_CHEMICAL|",
               "FERROPTOSIS|",
               "NEGATIVE_REGULATION_OF_FERROPTOSIS|",
               "DOPAMINE")) ~
        "Immune & Inflammatory\nResponse",

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 6 — Protein Processing &
      # Cellular Trafficking
      # Includes ER stress, lysosome,
      # extracellular transport
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("UNFOLDED_PROTEIN|",
               "PROTEASOME|",
               "RIBOSOME|",
               "TOPOLOGICALLY_INCORRECT|",
               "PROTEIN_INSERTION_INTO_ER|",
               "NUCLEAR_MEMBRANE|",
               "LYSOSOME|",
               "EXTRACELLULAR_TRANSPORT|",
               "ENDOPLASMIC_RETICULUM_STRESS|",
               "REGULATION_OF_ENDOPLASMIC")) ~
        "Protein Processing &\nCellular Trafficking",

      # ----------------------------------------
      # THEME 7 — Cytoskeletal & Ciliary Organization
      # Mostly upregulated pathways
      # ----------------------------------------
      str_detect(NAME,
        paste0("CILIUM|",
               "FLAGELLUM|",
               "CENTRIOLE|",
               "AXONEME|",
               "MICROTUBULE|",
               "MITOTIC_SPINDLE|",
               "PROTEIN_LOCALIZATION_TO_MICROTUBULE|",
               "PROTEIN_LOCALIZATION_TO_CILIUM")) ~
        "Cytoskeletal &\nCiliary Organization",

      # Catch anything missed
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

# ============================================
# VERIFY — Check every pathway got a theme
# ============================================

cat("=== THEME ASSIGNMENT SUMMARY ===\n")
print(table(sig_gsea_themed$theme))

cat("\n=== CHECK FOR UNASSIGNED PATHWAYS ===\n")
unassigned <- sig_gsea_themed %>%
  filter(theme == "Other") %>%
  select(NAME, database, direction)

if (nrow(unassigned) == 0) {
  cat("All pathways successfully assigned!\n")
} else {
  cat("WARNING:", nrow(unassigned),
      "pathways not assigned:\n")
  print(unassigned)
}

# ============================================
# VERIFY — Check themes make sense
# Print pathways per theme
# ============================================

cat("\n=== PATHWAYS BY THEME ===\n")
sig_gsea_themed %>%
  select(NAME, theme, direction,
         NES, database) %>%
  arrange(theme, direction, desc(abs(NES))) %>%
  group_by(theme) %>%
  group_walk(~ {
    cat("\n---", .y$theme, "---\n")
    print(as.data.frame(.x[,
      c("NAME", "NES",
        "direction", "database")]))
  })
```
```{r}
# Just run this part first
cat("=== THEME ASSIGNMENT SUMMARY ===\n")
print(table(sig_gsea_themed$theme))

cat("\n=== UNASSIGNED PATHWAYS ===\n")
unassigned <- sig_gsea_themed %>%
  filter(theme == "Other") %>%
  select(NAME, database, direction)
print(unassigned)
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# STEP 7 — Make a dot plot by theme
# ============================================

# Take top 3 pathways per theme per direction
top_pathways <- sig_gsea_themed %>%
  group_by(theme, direction) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(NES),
            n        = 3) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Clean up pathway names for plotting
  mutate(
    NAME_clean = str_replace_all(NAME, "_", " "),
    NAME_clean = str_to_title(
      str_remove(NAME_clean,
                 "^(Hallmark |KEGG |GOBP )")
    )
  )

# Dot plot
dot_plot <- ggplot(
  top_pathways,
  aes(x     = NES,
      y     = reorder(NAME_clean, NES),
      color = direction,
      size  = -log10(FDR.q.val))
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Upregulated"   = "blue",
      "Downregulated" = "red"
    )
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ theme,
    scales = "free_y",
    ncol   = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title  = "HTR-8/SVneo: GSEA Results by Biological Theme",
    x      = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y      = NULL,
    color  = "Direction",
    size   = "-Log10(FDR)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5,
                                size  = 12,
                                face  = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text  = element_text(face = "bold",
                                size = 9)
  )

print(dot_plot)

# Save dot plot
ggsave(
  filename    = "Figure3_GSEA_themes_dotplot.tiff",
  plot        = dot_plot,
  path        = here("output", "figures"),
  width       = 7,
  height      = 10,
  units       = "in",
  dpi         = 1200,
  device      = "tiff",
  compression = "lzw"
)
```
